articles.title: State of California Secures 20K-SF Office at 1180 Avenue of the Americas
articles.narrative: The State of California has secured a 20,000-square-foot office lease at 1180 Avenue of the Americas in Midtown Manhattan, relocating its Franchise Tax Board's field office from a nearby building. This transaction is part of a recent leasing surge at the Northwood Investors-owned tower, which has seen approximately 60,000 square feet of new leases and expansions since late April. Other recent deals include a law firm's expansion and new leases by a market research company and a staffing agency, underscoring the building's appeal due to its Midtown location and amenities.

data_points.address: 1180 Avenue of the Americas, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: 22-story Midtown office tower located between West 46th and West 47th streets.
data_points.project_notes: The tenant previously occupied an office at 1212 Avenue of the Americas.

articles.market_intelligence: Office rents in the Avenue of the Americas and Rockefeller Center submarket averaged $94.53 per square foot in the first quarter of 2026.

articles.title: Shirley Aninias School Signs 23K-SF Lease at 30 Wall Street
articles.narrative: The Shirley Aninias School, a private special education program, has signed a 15-year lease for 23,000 square feet across two floors at 30 Wall Street in New York City's Financial District. This transaction represents the largest lease ever signed at the property and will serve as a relocation and expansion for the school. The space will feature an internal staircase and a dedicated ground-floor entrance, creating a self-contained environment tailored for the school's neurodiverse students and staff.

data_points.address: 30 Wall Street, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: The leased space includes an internal staircase connecting the second and third floors and a dedicated ground-floor entrance, providing a self-contained environment.
data_points.project_notes: The Shirley Aninias School is relocating and expanding from its previous location at 291 Broadway in Tribeca.

articles.market_intelligence: Lower Manhattan is experiencing strong demand from organizations requiring more than traditional office space, particularly schools seeking dedicated and highly functional environments.

articles.title: Trump Administration Selects Development Team for $8B Penn Station Rebuild
articles.narrative: The Trump administration has selected Penn Transformation Partners, a joint venture between Halmar and Skanska, as the master developer for the $8 billion redevelopment of New York Penn Station. The plan aims to modernize the transit hub, increase track capacity, and revamp retail spaces while keeping Madison Square Garden in its current location with a refreshed facade. This federal takeover of the project follows years of stalled efforts by New York state to revitalize the station.

data_points.notable_features: Includes a major rebuild of the transit hub, increased track capacity, revamped retail, and fresh cladding for Madison Square Garden.
data_points.project_notes: The Trump administration took control of the redevelopment from New York state and the MTA in April 2025. USDOT announced $4.7 billion in funding for Northeast Corridor transit improvements in April 2026.

articles.title: CBRE’s Scott Schnuckel On Why Retail Is Back in Favor
articles.narrative: CBRE's managing director of retail for the Americas, Scott Schnuckel, discusses the resurgence of the retail real estate sector, attributing its strength to record-low vacancy rates and minimal new construction. He notes that the landlord community is benefiting from a long-term trend of limited new shopping center development, coupled with the demolition of older malls and a growth in retail brands, particularly value-oriented mass merchants, grocers, off-price retailers, and food and beverage concepts. While rent growth has been modest due to long-term lease contracts, investor demand is increasing, with retail becoming a favored sector for large funds seeking stability, especially for grocery-anchored and well-executed mixed-use projects. Schnuckel highlights that new construction is primarily occurring in high-growth Southern and West Coast markets like Phoenix, Texas, and Florida, where population expansion supports the development of new suburbs and incremental business for retailers.

people.name: Scott Schnuckel
people.title: Managing Director of Retail for the Americas

articles.title: Build-to-Rent Investors Catch a Break in U.S. House Bill
articles.narrative: The U.S. House of Representatives has approved a revised bipartisan housing affordability bill, the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, which removes a contentious provision from the Senate version that would have mandated institutional investors sell build-to-rent (BTR) single-family homes to individual buyers after seven years. Commercial real estate industry groups, including CREFC and the MBA, advocated against the divestment clause, arguing it was impractical and would deter capital investment in single-family rental developments, thereby hindering housing supply. The House's more deliberate approach, which clarifies misconceptions about institutional homeownership, is seen as an improvement by industry stakeholders, who emphasize the need to avoid disrupting the broader rental housing market.

people.name: Elizabeth Warren
people.title: ranking member of the Senate Banking, Housing and Urban Affairs Committee

articles.title: Walker & Dunlop Arranges $128.5M Acquisition Loan for Houston Apartment Complex
articles.narrative: Walker & Dunlop has arranged a $128.5 million acquisition loan for The Arno, a 168-unit apartment complex in Houston's River Oaks neighborhood. The financing was secured through Hudson Bay Capital on behalf of Sade Real Estate, facilitating the acquisition of this multifamily asset.

articles.title: Mesa West Provides $47M Loan for Refinancing of South Austin Multifamily Property
articles.narrative: Mesa West Capital has provided a $47 million loan for the refinancing of Bishop Momo, a 274-unit multifamily property in South Austin. The apartment complex, delivered in 2024, is located within the St. Elmo District, a large mixed-use redevelopment. Newmark arranged the debt for the borrower, United Properties.

data_points.notable_features: Located within the St. Elmo District, a 275-acre mixed-use redevelopment of a former industrial site. Includes 5,835 square feet of ground-floor retail space.

articles.title: PAGEWOOD Breaks Ground on Adaptive Reuse Project in Downtown Houston
articles.narrative: PAGEWOOD has broken ground on the first phase of East Blocks, an adaptive reuse project in Houston's East Downtown neighborhood. This initial phase involves converting two 15,000-square-foot warehouses into a mixed-use district of retail, office, and green spaces. The project is a partnership with Wile Interests and is expected to deliver in August.

data_points.address: 1107 Hutchins St. / 2202 Dallas St., Houston, TX
data_points.notable_features: Adaptive reuse project transforming a 10-block stretch of mid-20th century warehouses into a walkable district.

articles.title: Finial Group Negotiates Sale of Three Industrial Buildings Totaling 19,500 SF in West Houston
articles.narrative: Finial Group has brokered the sale of three industrial buildings totaling 19,500 square feet in West Houston. The transaction involved an undisclosed seller and an undisclosed buyer, with the sales price also remaining confidential.

articles.title: Piazza Auto Group Breaks Ground on 270-Unit Multifamily Project in Ardmore, Pennsylvania
articles.narrative: Piazza Auto Group, in partnership with Radnor Property Group, has broken ground on The Plaza at Ardmore, a 270-unit multifamily project in Ardmore, Pennsylvania. The development will also include 30,000 square feet of ground-floor retail space. Financing for the project is being provided by Bank OZK and Affinius Capital, with completion anticipated in early 2028.

data_points.notable_features: Amenities will include a pool, outdoor grilling and dining stations, a fitness center, and dedicated coworking and private study spaces.

articles.title: The Doran Group begins construction 128-unit seniors housing community in Hudson
articles.narrative: The Doran Group has commenced construction on Linden House – Hudson, a 128-unit seniors housing community in Hudson, Wisconsin. The project, which also secured $26 million in construction financing from Veridian Credit Union, represents the launch of the developer's second senior housing brand, targeting regional hubs and growing smaller cities in the Upper Midwest. The community is designed to offer upscale living with a variety of amenities and larger one- and two-bedroom homes.

data_points.notable_features: Offers larger one- and two-bedroom homes ranging from 690 to 1,380 square feet, private outdoor balconies, and underground heated parking. Amenities include an outdoor central terrace with a pool, clubroom, wellness center, and pet run and spa.
data_points.project_notes: Leasing is expected to begin in late 2026.

articles.title: JLL: Commercial real estate investors are back in spend mode
articles.narrative: Commercial real estate investors are increasingly confident and actively deploying capital, with a notable shift towards markets exhibiting strong long-term fundamentals such as population growth, infrastructure investment, and diversified economies. This trend is evidenced by a significant increase in global and U.S. transaction volumes in Q1 2026, with specific U.S. markets like San Francisco, Chicago, and Atlanta experiencing substantial surges in investment activity. Investors are prioritizing future growth potential over immediate yields, leading to a fundamental change in capital allocation strategies. The office sector, in particular, has shown a strong rebound, leading investment-sales growth globally and in the U.S., driven by large transactions and limited new supply.

people.name: Danny Finkle
people.title: Executive Managing Director and Investment Sales Platform Leader for JLL Capital Markets Americas

articles.title: Marcus & Millichap closes sale of 242-unit self-storage property in Janesville
articles.narrative: Marcus & Millichap has brokered the sale of Lark Self Storage, a 242-unit self-storage facility in Janesville, Wisconsin, for $2.86 million. The property, which includes both traditional self-storage units and RV/boat parking, was sold by a local seller to a local business owner. The new ownership plans to operate the facility as Interstate Secure Storage.

data_points.address: 3031 E. County Rd. O, Janesville, WI
data_points.notable_features: Includes 176 non-climate-controlled self-storage units and 66 RV and boat storage parking spaces. Located along Interstate 90 with access to Madison, Milwaukee, and Chicago.
data_points.project_notes: Recent updates were completed in 2025. The property will be rebranded as Interstate Secure Storage under new ownership.

articles.title: Milwaukee’s industrial market entering a period of steady growth? That’s what Marcus & Millichap is predicting
articles.narrative: Milwaukee's industrial market is anticipated to enter a period of steady growth, driven by strengthening tenant demand and increasing investor interest, according to a new report from Marcus & Millichap. The market is transitioning from a supply-driven environment, where significant new construction had pushed vacancy rates higher, towards a more balanced state. The report highlights easing vacancy pressures, robust demand for smaller industrial buildings, and the positive impact of infrastructure investments around Milwaukee Mitchell International Airport as key drivers for this optimistic outlook. A projected slowdown in new construction, with completions expected to fall by 35% in 2026, is also expected to contribute to declining vacancy and support rent growth.

people.name: Todd Lindblom
people.title: Senior Managing Director and Market Leader

articles.market_intelligence: More than 4 million square feet of industrial space was vacated during the first half of 2025. Demand for industrial buildings under 50,000 square feet remains strong, with vacancy levels below market averages. Institutional investment activity in Milwaukee's industrial sector is increasing, with a growing number of properties trading above $10 million, particularly in Milwaukee and Waukesha counties.

articles.title: Adolfson & Peterson Construction wraps interior build-out of Arlington children’s clinic
articles.narrative: Adolfson & Peterson Construction has completed the interior build-out of a new 6,450-square-foot pediatric primary care clinic for Cook Children's in Arlington, TX. The facility, which began construction in August 2025, features 15 exam rooms, treatment and laboratory spaces, and a waiting area with a playroom, enhancing access to pediatric care in the rapidly expanding Arlington community.

data_points.notable_features: Includes 15 exam rooms, dedicated treatment and laboratory spaces, and a waiting area with a playroom.
data_points.project_notes: Construction began in August 2025.

articles.title: Scion Group, Ares buy Harrison Street’s $910M student housing portfolio
articles.narrative: A joint venture between Scion Group and Ares Management has acquired a 12-property student housing portfolio from Harrison Street Asset Management for $910 million. The portfolio comprises 7,578 beds across a dozen college towns, with properties located near major universities such as Auburn University, Arizona State University, Ohio State University, and Washington State University. This acquisition represents a significant investment in the student housing sector, which is experiencing steady demand with rising college enrollment and strong pre-leasing activity, despite a deceleration in rent growth.

data_points.project_notes: The portfolio traded for approximately $120,000 per bed.

articles.market_intelligence: College enrollment increased by 1.8% nationally in Fall 2025. Pre-leasing rates for student housing are outpacing last year's figures. Average student housing rents reached $925 per bed in February, representing a 0.4% year-over-year increase, a deceleration from the 3.5% growth observed in February 2025.

articles.title: Google’s listings experiment adds pressure to fractured MLS landscape
articles.narrative: Google is re-testing a program to display home listings directly in its search results, partnering with AI-enabled brokerage HouseCanary. This initiative, which involves direct agreements with Multiple Listing Services (MLSes) like California Regional MLS, San Diego MLS, and My State MLS, as well as brokerages such as eXp Realty, signals a significant shift in listing syndication. The move is seen as a direct challenge to established real estate portals like Zillow, Realtor.com, and Homes.com, which have historically relied on MLS data feeds. Industry experts suggest that Google's immense consumer reach could disrupt the current portal models, especially as the MLS landscape becomes more fractured and brokerages increasingly withhold listings from immediate MLS syndication.

articles.title: Newmark asks court to compel $3M from Nightingale co-founder
articles.narrative: Newmark is seeking to compel Simon Singer, co-founder of Nightingale Properties, to provide financial records to satisfy a $3 million judgment. The judgment stems from a 2023 settlement related to Newmark's 2021 acquisition of Nightingale Realty, where Singer guaranteed up to $3 million of an $11 million payment owed by Elie Schwartz, who later pleaded guilty to wire fraud. Newmark alleges Singer is obstructing collection efforts by withholding details on his and his wife's intertwined finances, while Singer's legal team argues the judgment may be invalid and questions Newmark's undisclosed relationship with Schwartz.

articles.title: HUD hands over $130M construction loan for Denver VA hospital adaptive reuse
articles.narrative: GM Development secured a $130 million construction loan from the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) for the adaptive reuse of the former Veterans Affairs hospital campus in Denver. The financing, arranged by Walker & Dunlop, will facilitate the conversion of a 10-story hospital building into 493 apartments with ground-floor retail. This project is part of a larger mixed-use redevelopment that will ultimately include over 1,400 housing units, additional retail, and office space, with a portion of the initial residential units designated as affordable housing.

data_points.notable_features: Adaptive reuse of a 10-story historic hospital building into apartments with retail space, part of a larger mixed-use development.
data_points.project_notes: The 8.3-acre campus was previously acquired by GM Development in 2022 for approximately $41 million. About 8% of the 493 adapted hospital units will be reserved for tenants earning 60% of area median income or below. The hospital conversion is expected to be the first phase of the project.

companies_people.label: LENDER
companies_people.firm_name: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

articles.title: PolicyPro: J-51 reboot flops for rent-regulated landlords, boosts co-ops and condos
articles.narrative: The New York State Legislature is poised to approve a reboot of the J-51 property tax abatement program, extending it through 2036 and broadening eligibility for co-ops and condos. While the revamped program increases the benefit cap to 100% of reasonable rehabilitation costs and mandates biennial updates to cost schedules, it maintains the 50% affordability requirement for rental buildings, effectively excluding many rent-stabilized properties. This outcome is seen as a significant disappointment for owners of rent-regulated buildings, who sought a broader overhaul to include properties with at least 90% rent-regulated units. Conversely, co-ops and condos will benefit from a raised assessed value threshold for eligibility, which will also be indexed to inflation, and a cap on abatements at 50% of annual property tax, a limit not expected to deter participation.

companies_people.label: OTHER
companies_people.firm_name: Co-ops and Condos United of New York

articles.title: Hotel union deal puts cleaners on track for $100K salaries
articles.narrative: A tentative eight-year labor agreement between the Hotel and Gaming Trades Council and the New York City hotel industry is set to significantly increase wages for nearly 30,000 workers across more than 250 hotels. The deal, which includes over 50 percent average wage increases, will see housekeepers' hourly pay rise from approximately $40 to over $61 by 2034, enabling them to earn over $100,000 annually by the sixth year of the contract. This agreement comes as hotel owners already face rising operating costs from the Safe Hotels Act and a constrained new supply pipeline due to strict permitting rules, with industry leaders acknowledging the deal's substantial impact on their bottom line but deeming it necessary to avert a strike before the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

companies_people.label: OTHER
companies_people.firm_name: Hotel Association of New York City

articles.market_intelligence: Manhattan's average hotel room rates were almost $334 in 2025, the highest among major U.S. markets. New York City had 204 union hotels out of 785 total in 2025. Hotel occupancy rates are in the 85 percent range.

articles.title: Mamdani targets Brooklyn corridors for major housing push
articles.narrative: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's administration is initiating its first significant neighborhood rezoning effort, the "South of Prospect Plan," in Brooklyn. This plan targets commercial corridors along Coney Island and McDonald avenues, aiming to allow for taller buildings and thousands of new apartments. The initiative is a key part of the city's strategy for transit-oriented development, leveraging the area's proximity to subway lines and the proposed Interborough Express light rail. The Department of City Planning has begun public engagement, with a draft proposal expected next year, as the city seeks to address its housing shortage and create more affordable units.

data_points.notable_features: Targets commercial corridors along Coney Island and McDonald avenues, near multiple subway lines and the proposed Interborough Express light rail project.
data_points.project_notes: The rezoning aims to create thousands of housing units and align future growth with the IBX corridor. The Department of City Planning expects to release a draft proposal in 2027.

articles.title: Saunders partners with Premier Estate Properties to link ultralux markets
articles.narrative: Saunders & Associates, a Hamptons-based brokerage, and Premier Estate Properties, a South Florida firm, have formed a strategic partnership to connect their respective luxury real estate markets. This alliance will facilitate referral sharing, listing information exchange, and joint networking events, aiming to expand their reach in an increasingly consolidated industry. The collaboration leverages the strong symbiotic relationship between the Hamptons and Palm Beach, which are key markets for high-net-worth individuals.

articles.title: RealPage rent-fixing settlements pass $200M (and counting)
articles.narrative: Multiple landlords have reached settlement agreements totaling over $218 million in the ongoing class-action lawsuit alleging a rent-fixing scheme involving RealPage's YieldStar software. These settlements, disclosed in U.S. District Court filings, require the landlords to cease using nonpublic rental information from RealPage databases for pricing and prohibit them from using the software to set rental prices. The litigation against RealPage and various landlords began after a 2022 investigative article highlighted how the software's algorithm could potentially inflate rents and suppress competition. While the settling firms deny liability, the Department of Justice also pursued its own suit against RealPage, which was settled in November with commitments for platform changes and independent oversight.

articles.title: South Florida’s top deals: Publix drops $78M in Boynton Beach
articles.narrative: A subsidiary of the supermarket chain Publix acquired the Fountains of Boynton shopping center in Boynton Beach for $78 million. The property, anchored by a Publix store, was previously purchased by Union Investment in 2021 for $79.5 million. This transaction represents a significant commercial deal in South Florida.

data_points.address: 6545, 6627, 6661 and 6667 Boynton Beach Boulevard and 9811 South Job Road, Boynton Beach, FL
data_points.notable_features: Anchored by a Publix supermarket.
data_points.project_notes: The property was previously acquired by Union Investment in 2021 for $79.5 million.

articles.title: Waterfront Miami Beach mansion of late Broadway legend sells for $32M
articles.narrative: The waterfront Miami Beach mansion of the late Broadway legend Harold Prince has been sold for $31.5 million by his daughter, Daisy Chaplin. The 9,000-square-foot estate, built in 1937 on a 0.8-acre lot with 150 feet of bay frontage, is expected to be redeveloped. This transaction continues a trend of high-value sales on North Bay Road.

data_points.address: 6000 North Bay Road, Miami Beach, FL
data_points.notable_features: Waterfront property with 150 feet of bay frontage, likely to be redeveloped.
data_points.project_notes: Property was originally purchased by Harold Prince in 1987 for $1.7 million. Daisy Chaplin bought out her brother's interest in 2021 for $10 million.

articles.title: “We cannot fall so far behind”: Controversial 5-story jewelry store project sails past approval
articles.narrative: Fort Lauderdale commissioners have unanimously approved the rezoning and site plan for Weston Jewelers' controversial five-story flagship store on Las Olas Boulevard. The Arquitectonica-designed glass and wood panel building, which will rise 74 feet, faced opposition from residents concerned about the rezoning of a residential lot to commercial, the building's height, and its lack of on-site parking. Despite these concerns, the project is seen by supporters and city officials as crucial for downtown Fort Lauderdale's growth and competitiveness with neighboring markets.

data_points.address: 221-229 Southeast 12th Avenue and 1117-1121 East Las Olas Boulevard, Fort Lauderdale, FL
data_points.notable_features: Arquitectonica-designed glass and wood panel building; will include spaces for luxury brands like Rolex, Cartier, Omega, Breitling, and Chopard, as well as independent jewelers and a bridal section. The fourth floor will house a Dikes family office.
data_points.project_notes: The project will have zero on-site parking spots, with 100 off-site spaces secured through letters of intent with First Presbyterian Church (40 spaces), Demko Parking (40 spaces), and Moss Construction (20 spaces). Staff members will be prohibited from parking in public lots. One of the two lots was rezoned from residential to commercial.

articles.title: Dingle brothers sell waterfront spec mansion for $75M in Boca Raton record
articles.narrative: SRD Building, led by Steve and Scott Dingle, sold a newly constructed waterfront spec mansion in Boca Raton for $75 million, setting a new record for home sales in the city and within the exclusive Royal Palm Yacht & Country Club. The 18,300-square-foot estate, situated on a 0.8-acre corner lot with extensive water frontage, features eight bedrooms and a comprehensive wellness wing. The sale underscores the continued strength of South Florida's ultra-luxury residential market, with the broker noting that buyers at this price point seek premium, completed products.

data_points.address: 2500 East Maya Drive, Boca Raton, FL
data_points.notable_features: Located on a corner lot within the gated Royal Palm Yacht & Country Club with 292 feet of water frontage, featuring a wellness wing with a fitness center, club room, golf simulator, and game room.
data_points.project_notes: The 1.7-acre property was previously acquired by the Dingle siblings for $36 million in 2024 and subsequently split into two parcels for development.

articles.title: Sightwell scraps East Dundee lumberyard redevelopment plan
articles.narrative: Sightwell has withdrawn its plans for a mixed-use redevelopment of the former lumberyard at Heritage Station in East Dundee, citing economic constraints. The proposed $18 million project, which included retail, lodging, and townhomes, faced challenges with the allocated $2.5 million in special tax funding primarily covering site preparation rather than construction. Despite this setback, the village of East Dundee remains committed to the overall development vision for the area, including the ongoing construction of a performing arts center and plans to seek new bids for the lumberyard site's phased development.

data_points.project_notes: The village previously acquired the 110 Railroad Street site for $800,000 in 2023 and planned to sell it to Sightwell for $10. The project was to be funded by private investors and Tax Increment Financing from the village. The village is now actively searching for new bids to carry out the development plan in phases. Construction on a performing arts center nearby is continuing, and plans for a $1.5 million soundstage have been delayed to 2027.

articles.title: John Novak strengthens grip along Chicago River with two more purchases
articles.narrative: Novak Construction has acquired two additional properties at 1685 North Elston Avenue and 1907 North Mendell Street in Chicago, further expanding its holdings around the former Lincoln Yards megadevelopment site. These purchases follow Novak's earlier acquisition of 18.4 acres of the Lincoln Yards site for $34 million. The firm is strategically consolidating its position along the Chicago River, though specific development plans for these newly acquired satellite properties remain undisclosed.

data_points.project_notes: The acquired properties are satellite parcels near the former Lincoln Yards megadevelopment. Novak previously acquired 18.4 acres of the Lincoln Yards site for $34 million. The original Lincoln Yards project was planned as a $6 billion, 53-acre mixed-use district spanning 14.5 million square feet.

articles.title: Yitzy Klor pursuing third condo deconversion attempt at 200 North Dearborn with $98M offer
articles.narrative: A group of condo owners at 200 North Dearborn Street in Chicago are considering a $98 million bulk buyout offer from YK Investments, led by investor Yitzy Klor. This marks Klor's third attempt to deconvert the 310-unit building into rentals, following two previous failed offers through his firm, Strategic Properties of North America, due to financing issues. The current proposal, while higher than a recent offer, is being met with skepticism by some owners who experienced prolonged uncertainty during the prior failed attempts.

data_points.project_notes: This is the third deconversion attempt by Yitzy Klor for this property. The previous $95 million offer in 2022 by Strategic Properties of North America failed to close due to financing issues. The current offer includes $3.1 million for a buyer vote incentive and $1 million for unit owner upgrades. A city ordinance requires 85% of condo owners to agree to the sale for a bulk buyout to proceed.

companies_people.label: OWNER
companies_people.firm_name: Condo Board of 200 North Dearborn Street

articles.financing: Proposed financing includes a $108 million principal loan from Capital Funding Group at a 7.7% interest rate and $13 million in preferred equity from Crest Capital at a 15% interest rate, both with 36-month terms. The total investment, including closing costs and units already acquired, is estimated to exceed $136 million.

articles.title: Developer CityPads cashes out of Evanston apartments in sale to TLC
articles.narrative: TLC Management, a Chicago-area multifamily investor led by Stuart Handler, has acquired Tapestry Station, a 120-unit luxury apartment building in Evanston, IL, from developer CityPads for $38 million. This acquisition completes a 1031 exchange for TLC, following its $39.3 million sale of Whitechapel Apartments in Chicago. The transaction highlights Handler's strategy of divesting from Chicago properties to invest in suburban complexes near transit, while for CityPads, it marks a successful exit from a transit-oriented development that reportedly doubled investor equity.

data_points.notable_features: Luxury apartments with ground-level commercial space, indoor parking, rooftop deck, tenant lounges, and private workspaces, located near a train station.
data_points.project_notes: The development pays tribute to Vogue Fabrics, a longtime wholesaler that previously occupied the site, by displaying vintage Singer sewing machines in common areas.

articles.title: Chicago MLS cuts off data feed to Zillow
articles.narrative: Midwest Real Estate Data (MRED), the third-largest Multiple Listing Service in the U.S., has terminated its direct listing feed to Zillow, removing approximately 43,000 Chicago-area homes from the platform. This action is the culmination of a year-long dispute over Zillow's practice of blocking listings that were initially marketed through private channels, which MRED considers a violation of its licensing agreement prohibiting filtering listings based on the associated broker. MRED's CEO, Rebecca Jensen, emphasized the MLS's responsibility to consistently enforce its rules, stating that Zillow's selective exclusion breaches its agreement. Zillow, in response, has filed a federal antitrust lawsuit against MRED and Compass, alleging collusion to stifle competition, and argues that MRED's actions harm buyers, sellers, and agents by reducing access to listings.

articles.title: Master-planned community in store for Howe
articles.narrative: Cherukuru Investments, led by Westlake investor Ravi Cherukuru, has acquired a 180-acre undeveloped tract in Howe, TX, with plans to develop a mixed-use retail and residential master-planned community. The land, located at the northeast corner of FM 902 and Bennett Road, was sold by the Hightower Family Trust, which had owned the property for 140 years. This acquisition continues the trend of significant development along the US-75 corridor in Dallas' far north, driven by factors such as the Texas Instruments semiconductor facility expansion in nearby Sherman.

data_points.notable_features: Unimproved tract located outside Howe city limits but within the Howe Independent School District, approximately 23 miles south of Downtown McKinney and 58 miles from Downtown Dallas.
data_points.project_notes: The land's tax appraisal value doubled from $1.9 million in 2022 to $3.8 million. A municipal utility district petition was filed on April 28 for the development.

articles.market_intelligence: Greenfield sites along the US-75 corridor have experienced significant appreciation in recent years. Commercial development in the area includes Texas Instruments' $30 billion semiconductor facility expansion in Sherman, which is attracting residential investment throughout Grayson County.

articles.title: Endeavor points with bird dog certainty toward “Dog’s Head” development potential
articles.narrative: The Austin City Council is considering the annexation of a 2,614-acre parcel known as the "Dog's Head" into city limits, which would enable future mixed-use development by Endeavor Real Estate Group. The project is anticipated to generate $3.5 billion in property tax revenue for the city over three decades. The development is planned to include residential, commercial, and industrial components, with a commitment to affordable housing and a public trail along the Colorado River.

data_points.notable_features: Located directly east of Highway 183 and north of Austin-Bergstrom International Airport, featuring a dog head-shaped bend in the Colorado River.
data_points.project_notes: The site is currently outside Austin city limits in an unincorporated extraterritorial jurisdiction. The development agreement includes commitments for affordable housing and a public trail along the river.

articles.title: Foreclosures tick up in stubbornly expensive Austin
articles.narrative: Austin is experiencing a notable increase in home foreclosures, leading the nation last month with a 199% year-over-year spike in filings. This trend is attributed to employment issues and depreciation in home values, particularly impacting first-time homebuyers who purchased with low down payments in 2022 when prices peaked. Despite the rise, market experts suggest that foreclosures still represent a relatively small share of the overall market and reflect broader national housing trends rather than a localized crisis, as Austin remains one of the most overvalued housing markets in the country.
articles.market_intelligence: Austin's housing market is considered one of the most overvalued in the country, trailing only Newark, NJ, and the Philadelphia area, according to Fitch Ratings. While some sub-sectors like condos, certain luxury neighborhoods, and outlying suburbs have seen significant distress, home prices within Austin city limits have largely held flat.

articles.title: Bank OZK expanding in Houston by nearly tripling office space
articles.narrative: Bank OZK is significantly expanding its office footprint in Houston, nearly tripling its space at Brookhollow Central III from 15,000 square feet to 40,000 square feet. This expansion, expected to finalize in the fourth quarter of 2026, reflects the bank's strong growth in the Houston market, particularly within its corporate and institutional banking group. The move will also accommodate the addition of 15 new employees, increasing its local workforce from approximately 45 to 60.

data_points.project_notes: Bank OZK's original lease was for 7,800 square feet before a prior expansion to 15,000 square feet.

people.name: Jake Munn
people.title: President of Corporate and Institutional Banking

articles.market_intelligence: The Houston and Dallas economies are experiencing significant growth, particularly in the commercial and industrial sectors. Bank OZK issued $3 billion in real estate construction loans in 2023, exceeding its closest competitors by over $1 billion.

articles.title: Swenson switches to affordable apartments at downtown San Jose site
articles.narrative: Swenson has submitted an updated proposal for a seven-story, 272-unit apartment complex at 860 West San Carlos Street in San Jose, pivoting from an earlier market-rate plan to a 100% affordable housing project. The development will include units for low- and moderate-income households, with a significant portion designated for extremely-low-income tenants. The change in plans reflects the financial infeasibility of the prior market-rate proposal, with the developer aiming to break ground by late summer.

data_points.address: 860 West San Carlos Street, San Jose, CA
data_points.notable_features: 100% affordable housing project with units for low- and moderate-income households, located within walking distance of Diridon Station.
data_points.project_notes: The 2.0-acre site was previously acquired by Swenson and Republic Urban Properties from the Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority. An earlier 2022 proposal for a 12-story, 263-unit market-rate apartment project with ground-floor retail was deemed financially infeasible.

articles.title: Pac Heights’ Apple orchard grows with another exec mansion buy
articles.narrative: Apple executive Mike Fenger has acquired a historic mansion at 2649 Green Street in San Francisco's Pacific Heights neighborhood for $13 million. The transaction highlights the continued strength of the city's luxury housing market, particularly in Pacific Heights, which has seen a significant recovery in high-end sales.

data_points.notable_features: Historic mansion with six bedrooms, seven bathrooms, and Golden Gate Bridge views.

articles.market_intelligence: Sales of homes priced at $10 million or more in San Francisco jumped over 60 percent year over year in April. Pacific Heights has led the city's pricing leaderboard this year, with recent eight-figure sales including 2350 Broadway ($29.9 million), 2830 Pacific Avenue ($27.5 million), and 2606 Jackson Street ($24 million).

articles.title: YIMBY movement split on who should be CA’s next governor
articles.narrative: The YIMBY (Yes In My Backyard) movement in California is divided on which candidate to endorse in the upcoming gubernatorial race, despite housing being a central issue for all leading candidates. While YIMBY Action, the grassroots arm, endorsed Tom Steyer due to his pledge to build 1 million homes and his willingness to take bold stances, other factions within the movement, including California YIMBY, expressed reservations about his progressive politics and opposition to greenfield development. The split highlights the diverse political ideologies within the pro-housing coalition, ranging from socialists to conservative developers, and their differing priorities beyond just housing production.

articles.title: 22-bedroom East Bay estate trades for near-record price
articles.narrative: The Fieldhaven Estate, a 22-bedroom mansion in Alamo, CA, has sold for $21.5 million, nearing a record price for Contra Costa County. The 21-acre property, originally built by tech co-founder David Duffield, was purchased by an unnamed East Bay family. The sale reflects a strong luxury market in the East Bay, driven by an influx of wealth from the booming artificial intelligence sector in San Francisco and Silicon Valley.

data_points.notable_features: Features include a geese fountain from the 1800s, a tree house with a 75-foot rope suspension bridge, an aviary, and a 20-vehicle garage.
data_points.project_notes: The property was originally built by David Duffield in 2012 at a reported cost of $135 million. J. Taylor Crandall acquired it in 2020 for $19 million and invested $7 million in upgrades.

articles.market_intelligence: The luxury market in the East Bay is experiencing an influx of money from San Francisco and Silicon Valley, largely attributed to the booming artificial intelligence sector, leading to increased home prices and a mansion shortage in San Francisco.

articles.title: Equity Residential, AvalonBay to Merge for $52B Apartment REIT
articles.narrative: Apartment REITs Equity Residential and AvalonBay Communities, Inc. have announced an all-stock merger of equals, creating a combined company with a pro forma equity market capitalization of approximately $52 billion and a total enterprise value of approximately $69 billion. The new entity will manage over 180,000 rental apartments and have nearly 11,000 units in its construction pipeline. Benjamin Schall, current president and CEO of AvalonBay, will lead the combined company, which will maintain dual headquarters in Chicago and Arlington, VA. The merger is anticipated to close in the second half of 2026, subject to shareholder approvals.

data_points.notable_features: Combined company will have nearly 11,000 units in its construction pipeline.

articles.title: Colliers Closes 208K-SF Industrial Facility Sale in Ohio
articles.narrative: Colliers has facilitated the sale of Innovation Industrial Park Building 2, a 208,000-square-foot Class A industrial facility in Rossford, Ohio. The property, delivered in 2022 and fully leased, was acquired by Glen Una Management Company from seller Scannell Properties. This transaction highlights sustained investor interest in newly constructed industrial assets with strong tenancy and potential for rent growth, particularly in key Midwest logistics corridors.

data_points.notable_features: Class A industrial facility with immediate access to Interstate 75 and Interstate 80/90.

articles.title: Machinery Company Acquires Suburban Chicago Office, Warehouse Space
articles.narrative: WSI Machinery, a provider of woodworking machinery and supplies, has acquired a 28,300-square-foot industrial building in Wauconda, Illinois. The company plans to renovate both the office and warehouse spaces to support its continued growth and operational expansion. This acquisition also provides future expansion capability through the opportunity to lease a secondary unit.

data_points.notable_features: Free-standing facility with 19-foot clear height, two exterior docks, two drive-in doors (12’ x 14’), and a triple catch basin system.

articles.title: Law Firm Jones Jones Relocates within Financial District
articles.narrative: Law firm Jones Jones LLC has signed a 10-year, nine-month lease for 5,311 square feet at 44 Wall St. in Manhattan's Financial District. The firm is relocating from 5 Hanover Square, which is slated for residential conversion, choosing to remain in Lower Manhattan due to its accessibility, transportation options, and value for office tenants. The move reflects a broader trend in the Financial District where older office properties are being converted to residential use, prompting office tenants to seek quality space that offers both location and efficiency.

data_points.address: 44 Wall St., New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Located in Manhattan's Financial District, offering extensive transportation options, dining, and retail amenities.
data_points.project_notes: Law firm is relocating from 5 Hanover Square, which is planned for residential conversion.

articles.title: Chicago Commercial Real Estate Awards Event Raises $1M for Hunger Relief
articles.narrative: The 38th Annual Chicago Commercial Real Estate Awards, presented by Associated Bank, recently convened over 800 commercial real estate professionals at The Old Post Office. The event successfully raised $1.1 million for the Greater Chicago Food Depository, marking it as the Food Depository's largest annual fundraiser. This long-standing partnership, initiated in 1989, has generated millions of dollars to combat hunger in Chicago and Cook County.

articles.title: Impact Funds Lose Steam With Investors, But Study Shows Their Buildings Perform Better
articles.narrative: Despite a record high in withdrawals from socially conscious investment funds in Q4, a new study indicates that multifamily impact investing, particularly in affordable housing, demonstrates superior financial performance and stability. The Multifamily Impact Council and NYU Stern School of Business found that strategies like rent-splitting and housing support plans increase tenant stability, reduce vacancy loss, and boost net operating income. This performance is particularly attractive in a softening rental market characterized by declining rents and rising vacancies, suggesting that impact-oriented strategies are proving to be a durable business model with both social and economic benefits.

companies_people.label: OTHER
companies_people.firm_name: New York University Stern School of Business

articles.market_intelligence: The U.S. apartment market is currently experiencing a supply glut, leading to a 1.7% year-over-year decline in average rents in April and a 7.2% vacancy rate. New York City's pension funds plan to invest $4B in affordable homes over the next four years due to a record number of rent-burdened households. The expansion of the low-income housing tax credit is also driving investment in affordable units.

articles.title: Yardi: Miami Has The Nation's Tightest Office Market
articles.narrative: Miami's office market has emerged as the tightest in the nation, with its vacancy rate dropping to 12.5% as of April 2026, significantly below the national average. This strong performance is attributed to robust growth in office-using employment, particularly within the financial activities sector, which has helped sustain demand despite tech layoffs. The influx of new-to-market companies and expansions by existing tenants, including a major e-commerce giant, have further fueled this trend, leading to Miami's average listing price of $58.41 per SF, making it the second most expensive market in the country.
articles.market_intelligence: Miami's office market is the tightest among the top 25 metros. The city's average listing price is 77% higher than the national average. Finance, banking, insurance, and real estate sectors account for 20% of South Florida's leasing activity, while tech companies make up 37%. Asking rents in Coral Gables increased by 14% in Q1 over the previous year.

articles.title: Top Partners Industrial Broker Travis Land Dies At 45
articles.narrative: Travis Land, a prominent industrial broker and partner at Partners Real Estate, passed away at the age of 45 after a battle with cancer. Land was a top producer for the firm, achieving the No. 1 overall producer ranking in 2021, 2023, and 2024, and closing over 30M SF of transactions during his 22-year career. He was also a significant shareholder and board member, known for his sharp wit and insightful counsel.

articles.title: Burlington Office Building Sells For One-Third Its Prior Price
articles.narrative: K & L Realty Co. acquired the 116,000-square-foot office building at One Burlington Woods Drive in Burlington, MA, for $8.5 million. The sale represents a significant discount from its prior sale price in 2013, reflecting the broader decline in suburban office values. The property is 60% occupied, and its low acquisition cost is expected to enable the new owner to offer competitive rents, potentially attracting tenants from further out on Route 495.

data_points.notable_features: Located near the Burlington Mall and a major highway, includes a cafeteria and fitness center with showers, lockers, and towel service.
data_points.project_notes: Seller Griffith Properties acquired the building for $26.6 million in 2013. Renovated in 2011.

articles.market_intelligence: Office vacancy in the 128 North submarket, which includes Burlington, was 14% last quarter, lower than the 17.8% vacancy across the entire Route 128 corridor. However, leasing activity in the submarket was the lowest in the corridor, with approximately 73,000 SF of deals signed in Q1.
